description: To feel and express great happiness, to experience joy so full it must be expressed. 'Kela' names the inner surge of gladness—the heart lifting, spirits soaring, gratitude overflowing into expression. It is joy that cannot stay contained.
sound_symbolism: The word bursts with joy. 'Ke' begins with velar brightness, joy springing forth. Then 'la' flows with liquid release, gladness overflowing, expression inevitable.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an intransitive verb: 'mi kela' (I rejoice). Can take cause: 'mi lo wela themo kela' (I rejoice at/because of something good). More internal than pharuki (celebrate); kela is the feeling, pharuki is the communal marking.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Mudita—sympathetic joy—rejoices in others' happiness. 'Kela' supports this practice of finding joy in joy wherever it appears.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Expressing joy openly invites others into gladness. Rejoicing together multiplies happiness.
